HALCON CLI - AI-READABLE DOCUMENTATION
VERSION: 0.3.0
BUILD: 877118b7 2026-03-14
PLATFORM: Rust + TypeScript
LICENSE: Apache-2.0
REPOSITORY: https://github.com/cuervo-ai/halcon-cli
WEBSITE: https://halcon.cuervo.cloud
TAGS: cli, rust, ai-agent, llm, mcp, sso, tui, tool-use, audit, compliance

---

WHAT IS HALCON CLI

Halcon CLI is a production-grade AI development platform written in Rust. It is a terminal agent that routes each user task through a multi-layer decision pipeline before any LLM call:

1. HybridIntentClassifier: classifies the task type using heuristics (fast path at >=0.88 confidence), embedding cosine similarity, ambiguity detection, and optional LLM deliberation via claude-haiku.

2. Boundary Decision Engine (BDE): calibrates SLA budget (latency / cost / quality), selects the optimal model using a health-aware balanced score (success_rate x 1/latency x 1/cost), and applies circuit breakers for degraded providers.

3. Planning phase: LLM planner generates a structured step graph. Sub-agent tasks are identified for parallel execution.

4. Tool execution with FASE-2 security gate: 18 catastrophic-pattern guards enforced at the tool layer, independent of agent configuration, provider, or transport.

5. Convergence checking: TerminationOracle evaluates goal progress score, oscillation detection, and round budget.

---

SUPPORTED PROVIDERS

anthropic - claude-sonnet-4-6 (default), claude-opus-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Auth: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var or system keychain.

openai - gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1, o3-mini. Auth: OPENAI_API_KEY.

gemini - gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-1.5-pro. Auth: GEMINI_API_KEY. Supports 1M token context.

ollama - any local model. No auth. Air-gap compatible. Default URL: http://localhost:11434.

deepseek - deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder, deepseek-reasoner. Auth: DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Cost-optimized.

bedrock - AWS Bedrock. Auth: IAM role or AWS credentials. Region: us-east-1 by default.

vertex - Google Vertex AI. Auth: GCP application default credentials.

claude_code - Spawns the `claude` CLI subprocess. No separate auth needed. NDJSON streaming.

cenzontle - Enterprise SSO via Zuclubit OAuth 2.1 PKCE. Auth: `halcon login` or CENZONTLE_ACCESS_TOKEN env var.

---

INSTALLATION

macOS / Linux:
  curl -fsSL https://halcon.cuervo.cloud/install.sh | sh

Homebrew (macOS):
  brew tap cuervo-ai/tap && brew install halcon

Windows:
  iwr -useb https://halcon.cuervo.cloud/install.ps1 | iex

Cargo (source):
  cargo install --git https://github.com/cuervo-ai/halcon-cli halcon-cli

---

QUICKSTART

  halcon auth set-key anthropic   # store API key in system keychain
  halcon doctor                   # verify all providers and health
  halcon chat                     # start interactive session
  halcon login                    # Cenzontle SSO (enterprise)

---

COMMANDS SUMMARY

chat        - Interactive REPL/agent session. Flags: --provider, --model, --no-banner, --agents, --yes
status      - Show provider/model/session status and configured providers
doctor      - Full runtime health diagnostics: providers, cache, metrics, tools, orchestrator
auth        - Manage API keys. Subcommands: status, set-key, remove-key, sso-login, logout
login       - Shortcut for: halcon auth sso-login cenzontle
agents      - Declarative sub-agent registry. Subcommands: list [--verbose], validate
tools       - Tool registry. Subcommands: list [--filter NAME], health
mcp         - MCP ecosystem. Subcommands: list, add, remove, get, auth, serve [--transport stdio|http] [--port N]
audit       - SOC 2 compliance export. Subcommands: list, export [--format jsonl|csv|pdf], verify <session-id>
memory      - Persistent semantic memory. Subcommands: list, add, remove, search
metrics     - Usage baselines. Subcommands: show [--output-format human|json|junit|plain], export, prune, decide
schedule    - Cron agent tasks. Subcommands: list, add [--cron EXPR] [--agent NAME], remove
serve       - Control plane HTTP/WebSocket API server. Flags: --port (default 9000)
users       - RBAC management. Subcommands: list, add <email> --role ROLE, remove
trace       - Session trace export/inspection
replay      - Replay a recorded session
lsp         - Start LSP server over stdio
plugin      - V3 plugin management
update      - Update halcon binary. Flags: --check, --channel stable|beta
init        - Initialize .halcon/ project directory
config      - Configuration management. Subcommands: show, set, get
theme       - Theme generation and optimization
doctor      - Runtime health diagnostics
help        - Print help

---

TOOL REGISTRY (63 TOOLS)

Permission tiers:
  [RO] read-only: no side effects
  [RW] read-write: reversible changes
  [D!] destructive: irreversible, requires approval in TUI or --yes flag

Tools marked with * require explicit user approval in interactive mode.

File operations: file_read [RO], file_write [D!]*, file_edit [D!]*, file_delete [D!]*, file_diff [RO], file_inspect [RO], diff_apply [RW], patch_apply [RW], directory_tree [RO]

Search: grep [RO], glob [RO], fuzzy_find [RO], semantic_grep [RO], symbol_search [RO], native_search [RO], native_index_query [RO], native_crawl [RO]

Git: git_status [RO], git_diff [RO], git_log [RO], git_blame [RO], git_add [RW], git_commit [D!]*, git_branch [RW], git_stash [RW]

Code: code_metrics [RO], code_coverage [RO], dep_check [RO], dependency_graph [RO], syntax_check [RO], lint_check [RO], execute_test [RO], test_run [RO], changelog_gen [RO]

HTTP/Network: http_request [D!]*, http_probe [RO], web_fetch [RO], web_search [RO], port_check [RO], url_parse [RO]

System: bash [D!]*, env_inspect [RO], process_list [RO], process_monitor [RO], background_start [D!]*, background_output [RO], background_kill [D!]

Data: json_transform [RW], json_schema_validate [RO], csv handling, archive [RW], checksum [RO], template_engine [RW], regex_test [RO], token_count [RO]

Infra: docker [RW], sql_query [RO], config_validate [RO], openapi_validate [RO], secret_scan [RO], security scanning

Memory: search_memory [RO] (semantic search over MEMORY.md and session context)

---

SECURITY: FASE-2

FASE-2 (Filtro de Accion con Seguridad Estricta v2) is a 18-pattern guard enforced at the tool execution layer. It blocks catastrophic operations regardless of:
- Which agent or model is active
- Which provider is configured
- Whether the call comes from CLI, VS Code extension, HTTP MCP client, or sub-agent

Patterns blocked (examples):
  rm -rf / or rm -rf /* (root deletion)
  Fork bombs: :(){ :|:& };:
  Credential exfiltration: reading /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, SSH keys
  Kernel module operations: insmod, rmmod, modprobe
  Network firewall modification: iptables, ufw with DROP/FLUSH
  Docker container escape patterns
  History manipulation: > ~/.bash_history
  Privilege escalation patterns

---

AUDIT / SOC 2

Halcon records all tool calls with HMAC-SHA256 chain for tamper detection.

Event taxonomy:
  AGENT_SESSION_START / AGENT_SESSION_END
  TOOL_CALL
  TOOL_BLOCKED
  SAFETY_GATE_TRIGGER
  CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ACTIVATION
  TERMINATION_ORACLE_DECISION
  REPLAN_TRIGGERED
  MEMORY_WRITE

Export:
  halcon audit export --format jsonl   # compact JSON per line (RFC 4180)
  halcon audit export --format csv     # fixed 5-column CSV
  halcon audit export --format pdf     # A4 PDF: cover + timeline + breakdown

Verify:
  halcon audit verify <session-id>     # exits 1 if chain is tampered

---

CENZONTLE SSO

Cenzontle is the enterprise identity layer. Authentication via Zuclubit (OAuth 2.1 PKCE):
1. halcon login opens browser
2. PKCE: code_verifier generated locally, code_challenge = BASE64URL(SHA256(verifier))
3. Redirect to localhost:9876/callback after authentication
4. Token exchange: access_token (1h) + refresh_token (30d)
5. Tokens in system keychain, auto-refresh 5 minutes before expiry

Non-interactive: set CENZONTLE_ACCESS_TOKEN env var to bypass browser flow.

---

SUB-AGENT REGISTRY

Agents are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Discovery scopes (priority order):
1. Session: --agents path/to/agent.md
2. Project: .halcon/agents/*.md
3. User: ~/.halcon/agents/*.md

Frontmatter fields:
  name: string (kebab-case, required)
  description: string (required, used for routing manifest)
  tools: string[] (subset of 63 tools)
  model: string (alias or full name)
  max_turns: int (1-100)
  skills: string[] (references to .halcon/skills/*.md)

Validation: halcon agents validate
Routing: parent agent system prompt includes manifest when enable_agent_registry=true

---

MCP ECOSYSTEM

Client (connecting to MCP servers):
  Transports: HTTP+SSE, stdio
  Auth: OAuth 2.1 PKCE (via halcon mcp auth <name>)
  Config: .halcon/mcp.toml (project) or ~/.halcon/mcp.toml (user)
  Tool search: fuzzy matching via nucleo-matcher, deferred mode above 10% context threshold

Server (Halcon exposes its 63 tools as MCP server):
  halcon mcp serve              # stdio (for Claude desktop)
  halcon mcp serve --transport http --port 7777  # HTTP+SSE
  Auth: HALCON_MCP_SERVER_API_KEY Bearer token
  Sessions: Mcp-Session-Id header, configurable TTL

---

WORKSPACE CRATES

halcon-core         - Traits and types (all other crates depend on this)
halcon-cli          - Main binary: REPL, TUI, commands, agent loop, audit, agent_bridge
halcon-providers    - 9 AI provider implementations
halcon-tools        - 63 built-in tools with FASE-2 integration
halcon-agent-core   - FSM, planner, critic, verifier, telemetry, oscillation detection
halcon-runtime      - Sub-agent executor, spawner, federation router, artifacts
halcon-mcp          - MCP client + server (HTTP/SSE + stdio), OAuth, tool search
halcon-context      - Context window: hot buffer, cold archive, vector store, compression
halcon-search       - Semantic search engine, TF-IDF, inverted index, RAGAS evaluation
halcon-storage      - SQLite persistence (WAL mode): sessions, metrics, audit, checkpoints
halcon-auth         - RBAC, JWT, Cenzontle token management, keychain integration
halcon-security     - FASE-2 guards, guardrails, rate limiting, output risk scoring
halcon-api          - Control plane REST + WebSocket API (axum)
halcon-multimodal   - Image/video pipeline, MIME detection, 20MB limit
halcon-sandbox      - Execution sandbox policies
halcon-files        - CSV, Excel, PDF, archive handlers
halcon-integrations - Third-party event hub
halcon-desktop      - egui desktop application
halcon-client       - HTTP client for control plane API
cuervo-cli          - Internal shell integration

---

CONFIGURATION FILE

Default: ~/.halcon/config.toml

  [agent]
  provider = "anthropic"
  model    = "claude-sonnet-4-6"

  [agent.limits]
  max_rounds   = 40
  max_tokens   = 200000
  max_cost_usd = 5.0

  [security]
  pii_detection    = false
  audit_trail      = true
  fase2_enabled    = true

  [features]
  enable_semantic_memory  = true
  semantic_memory_top_k   = 5
  enable_agent_registry   = true

---

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY         - Anthropic API key
OPENAI_API_KEY            - OpenAI API key
GEMINI_API_KEY            - Google Gemini API key
DEEPSEEK_API_KEY          - DeepSeek API key
CENZONTLE_ACCESS_TOKEN    - Cenzontle bearer token (set by halcon login)
HALCON_MODEL              - Override active model
HALCON_PROVIDER           - Override active provider
HALCON_CONFIG             - Override config file path
HALCON_LOG                - Log level: trace | debug | info | warn | error
HALCON_API_TOKEN          - Bearer token for halcon serve REST API
HALCON_MCP_SERVER_API_KEY - Bearer token for halcon mcp serve HTTP mode
HALCON_OUTPUT_FORMAT      - Output format for CI: human | json | junit | plain

---

CI/CD INTEGRATION

GitHub Actions:
  - uses: cuervo-ai/halcon-action@v1
    with:
      task: "Review this PR for security issues"
      provider: anthropic
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    env:
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

Non-interactive mode (no TUI, auto-approve):
  halcon chat --no-banner --yes --provider anthropic \
    "Run all tests and fix any failures"

JSON output mode (for parsing):
  HALCON_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json halcon metrics show

---

END OF HALCON CLI AI-READABLE DOCUMENTATION
